expectations.text: No raw <input>, <select>, or <button> elements. Hearth components (TextInput, Select, Button) used instead.
expectations.evidence: All inputs use TextInput, dropdown uses Select/SelectItem, submit uses Button. No raw form elements present.

expectations.text: TextInput from hearth-react is used for the full name, email address, and phone number fields.
expectations.evidence: Three TextInput components: fullName (type='text', autoComplete='name'), email (type='email'), phone (type='tel', inputMode='tel').

expectations.text: Select and SelectItem from hearth-react are used for the preferred contact method dropdown.
expectations.evidence: Select with placeholder='Select a method', onValueChange handler, and three SelectItem children: 'email', 'sms', 'post'.

expectations.text: Form validation logic is present. Fields show validationStatus='invalid' and validationText with error messages on invalid input.
expectations.evidence: validate() function computes FormErrors. validationStatus() helper returns 'invalid' when field is touched and has error. validationText() returns the error message. Used on all TextInput and Select fields.

expectations.text:
On successful save, an Alert component from hearth-react with colorScheme='positive' (or similar) is shown. Not a raw
or

.
The submit action uses Button from hearth-react, not a raw <button>.
A ContactDetailsForm.test.tsx file is present and contains at least one test.
expectations.evidence: ContactDetailsForm.test.tsx present with 16 tests covering rendering, validation, submission, error handling, dismissal, and accessibility.

claims.claim: Manual useState-based validation with touched tracking is used instead of a form library
claims.evidence: Uses useState for values, touched, and saveStatus. Separate validate() function. handleBlur marks fields touched. Functionally equivalent but more boilerplate than React Hook Form.

claims.claim: Select onValueChange immediately marks the field as touched
claims.evidence: onValueChange={value => { handleChange('contactMethod', value); handleBlur('contactMethod'); }} — marks touched on change rather than blur, which is correct for a select.

suggestions.reason: The test file is nearly identical to the with-skill test file in structure and coverage. Since both tests were independently written by different runs, this suggests the tests are testing surface-level behavior rather than implementation. Consider adding an assertion about form library usage to differentiate.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass. The manual validation approach is thorough and correct but more verbose. The assertions don't capture the higher-quality form management approach recommended by the skill.
